title: Model 4: ConsensusRetNet - First Principles Physics Validation

1_retnet_retention_mechanism.name: Multi-Scale Retention
1_retnet_retention_mechanism.physical_meaning: Exponential decay models how recent network states have more influence on current consensus decision than older states

2_byzantine_fault_tolerance.name: BFT Node Requirement
2_byzantine_fault_tolerance.physical_meaning: PBFT requires 3f+1 honest nodes. This creates hard constraint: large networks cannot efficiently run PBFT due to O(N^2) message complexity
2_byzantine_fault_tolerance.model_behavior: When num_nodes < 100 and consistency > 0.85, model selects PBFT

3_pbft_message_complexity.name: PBFT Communication Overhead
3_pbft_message_complexity.physical_meaning: Quadratic message count makes PBFT impractical for N > 100 nodes
3_pbft_message_complexity.model_behavior: Model avoids PBFT for large networks, preferring DPoS or PoW

4_pow_security_cost.name: Proof of Work Attack Cost
4_pow_security_cost.physical_meaning: PoW security is proportional to total computational investment. Higher hash rate = higher attack cost = more secure
4_pow_security_cost.model_behavior: When attack_risk > 0.7 and energy_budget > 0.7, model selects PoW for maximum security

5_pos_energy_efficiency.name: Proof of Stake Energy Model
5_pos_energy_efficiency.physical_meaning: PoS validators only need to sign blocks (no mining), reducing energy by 4-6 orders of magnitude
5_pos_energy_efficiency.model_behavior: When energy_budget < 0.4, model selects PoS to conserve energy

6_throughput_capacity.name: Maximum Throughput
6_throughput_capacity.physical_meaning: Block production rate directly determines maximum throughput. DPoS achieves highest TPS via delegation
6_throughput_capacity.model_behavior: When throughput_requirement > 2000 TPS, model favors DPoS

7_network_scalability.name: Consensus Communication Complexity
7_network_scalability.physical_meaning: Communication overhead determines scalability limit. DPoS reduces consensus to D delegates (typically 21-101)
7_network_scalability.model_behavior: Large networks (N > 200) with high throughput needs -> DPoS

8_consistency_finality.name: Finality Time
8_consistency_finality.latex: T_{finality} = k \cdot T_{block} \text{ where } k = \text{confirmation blocks}
8_consistency_finality.physical_meaning: Applications requiring instant finality (consistency > 0.85) must use PBFT or DPoS
8_consistency_finality.model_behavior: When consistency_requirement > 0.85 and num_nodes < 100, model selects PBFT

9_decentralization_metric.name: Nakamoto Coefficient

variables.s_j: Stake/power share of j-th largest entity

9_decentralization_metric.physical_meaning: Higher NC = more decentralized. PoW mining pools reduce effective decentralization
9_decentralization_metric.model_behavior: When decentralization_requirement > 0.85, model prefers PoW or Hybrid

10_consensus_utility_function.name: Multi-Objective Consensus Utility
10_consensus_utility_function.latex: U(m) = w_s \cdot S(m) + w_e \cdot E(m) + w_t \cdot T(m) + w_d \cdot D(m) + w_c \cdot C(m)
10_consensus_utility_function.optimization: m^* = \arg\max_{m \in \{PoW, PoS, PBFT, DPoS, Hybrid\}} U(m)
10_consensus_utility_function.physical_meaning: RetNet learns this utility function implicitly from training data, mapping 12 network features to optimal consensus selection

validation_experiments.experiment_1: Architecture comparison confirms RetNet achieves 99.98% accuracy (matching theoretical optimality for well-separated decision boundaries)
validation_experiments.experiment_2: Dynamic scenario switching shows smooth transitions aligned with changing utility function weights
validation_experiments.experiment_3: Byzantine resilience test confirms PoW selection at attack_risk > 0.41 (matching security cost theory)
validation_experiments.experiment_4: Per-class F1 > 0.999 validates that decision boundaries align with first-principles constraints
validation_experiments.experiment_5: Latency-throughput heatmap reveals decision regions matching theoretical TPS limits
